title: chore: split Fireworks Neo key
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Adds `FIREWORKS_NEO_API_KEY` for Fireworks serverless text and embeddings.
- Keeps `FIREWORKS_API_KEY` for Flux on the current Fireworks account.
- Updates encrypted gen secrets for dev, staging, and production with both keys.
- Validated with Biome, typecheck, focused Vitest, direct Fireworks provider checks, and local gen requests.

gist.headline: Splitting the Fireworks
gist.blurb: We've split our Fireworks API keys to keep text and image generation running smoothly on their own dedicated channels.
gist.summary: Split the Fireworks API configuration to use a dedicated FIREWORKS_NEO_API_KEY for serverless text and embeddings, while keeping the original key for Flux image generation.
gist.impact: This internal infrastructure update ensures better rate limit management and reliability across different Fireworks-powered models.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot managing two different glowing keys or fireworks. One key for text (scrolls/books), one for images (pictures).
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot holding two glowing pixelated keys, one emitting soft text symbols and the other tiny pictures. A chunky retro server rack in the background glows with soft lime green (#ecf874) lights. Soft pastel gradient background, warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley.

image.prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot holding two glowing pixelated keys, one emitting soft text symbols and the other tiny pictures. A chunky retro server rack in the background glows with soft lime green (#ecf874) lights. Soft pastel gradient background, warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley.
